headline.purpose: Major view titles and exceptional empty states.

title.purpose: Card groups, dialog titles, and settings sections.

body.purpose: Friendly guidance, report labels, and field descriptions.

label.purpose: Fields, table headings, selected tabs, and compact actions.

shadows.name: low-overlay
shadows.purpose: Compact floating controls such as menus and tooltips.

shadows.name: dialog-overlay
shadows.purpose: Modal surfaces that must separate clearly from the obscured workspace.

motion.name: state-change
motion.purpose: Short hover, focus, disclosure, and status transitions; disabled under reduced motion.

motion.name: chart-update
motion.purpose: Charts change state without entrance or morph animation.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single clearest action in a local region.
components.css: .ds-button-primary { background:#1b264f; color:#fff; border:1px solid #1b264f; border-radius:3px; padding:9px 18px; font:700 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; cursor:pointer; transition:background 120ms ease,border-color 120ms ease; } .ds-button-primary:hover { background:#3544b1; border-color:#3544b1; } .ds-button-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #3544b1; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-button-primary:active { background:#171d5a; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A compact supporting action such as View all, Retry, or Close.
components.css: .ds-button-secondary { background:#f3f3f5; color:#1b264f; border:1px solid transparent; border-radius:3px; padding:9px 18px; font:700 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; cursor:pointer; transition:background 120ms ease,border-color 120ms ease; } .ds-button-secondary:hover { background:#e8eaf4; border-color:#d8d7d9; } .ds-button-secondary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #3544b1; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-button-secondary:active { background:#d8d7d9; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: A familiar Umbraco field with local focus and validation treatment.
components.css: .ds-field { display:grid; gap:6px; color:#060606; font:700 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; } .ds-field input { background:#fff; color:#060606; border:1px solid #d8d7d9; border-radius:3px; padding:9px 12px; font:400 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; } .ds-field input:hover { border-color:#a1a1a1; } .ds-field input:focus-visible { border-color:#3544b1; outline:2px solid #3544b1; outline-offset:1px; }

components.name: Status Tag
components.description: A text-backed connection or configuration status.
components.css: .ds-status-tag { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:6px; background:#eaf8f1; color:#075236; border:1px solid #82d6ae; border-radius:3px; padding:3px 9px; font:700 12px/18px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; } .ds-status-tag:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #3544b1; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: A flat reporting or settings surface separated by a quiet border.
components.css: .ds-card { background:#fff; color:#060606; border:1px solid #d8d7d9; border-radius:3px; padding:18px; font:400 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; } .ds-card h3 { margin:0 0 18px; font:400 21px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; } .ds-card p { margin:0; } .ds-card:focus-within { border-color:#a1a1a1; }

components.name: Metric Tab
components.description: The chart header combines a selectable metric, total, and previous-period comparison.
components.css: .ds-metric-tab { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr auto; gap:9px 18px; min-width:240px; background:#fff; color:#060606; border:0; border-bottom:3px solid #3544b1; padding:18px; text-align:left; font:700 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; cursor:pointer; } .ds-metric-tab strong { font-size:36px; line-height:1.1; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; white-space:nowrap; } .ds-metric-tab em { align-self:center; color:#c31d4c; font-style:normal; font-size:16px; white-space:nowrap; } .ds-metric-tab:hover { background:#f0f1fa; } .ds-metric-tab:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #3544b1; outline-offset:-2px; }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: A removable analytics filter whose value is the primary visible label.
components.css: .ds-filter-chip { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:9px; max-width:280px; background:#fff; color:#1b264f; border:1px solid #d8d7d9; border-radius:3px; padding:6px 9px; font:400 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; cursor:pointer; } .ds-filter-chip:hover { background:#f0f1fa; border-color:#a1a1a1; } .ds-filter-chip:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #3544b1; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-filter-chip:active { background:#e8eaf4; }

components.name: Breakdown Row
components.description: A right-aligned metric row with a proportional tonal bar and stable content inset.
components.css: .ds-breakdown-row { position:relative; display:grid; grid-template-columns:minmax(0,1fr) auto; gap:18px; padding:9px 12px; color:#060606; font:400 14px/21px Lato,Arial,sans-serif; overflow:hidden; } .ds-breakdown-row::before { content:""; position:absolute; inset:3px auto 3px 0; z-index:0; width:max(4px,var(--bar)); background:#f0f1fa; border-radius:3px; } .ds-breakdown-row span,.ds-breakdown-row strong { position:relative; z-index:1; } .ds-breakdown-row strong { font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; text-align:right; } .ds-breakdown-row:hover::before { background:#e8eaf4; } .ds-breakdown-row:focus-within { outline:2px solid #3544b1; outline-offset:-2px; }

narrative.overview: Web Analytics is a precise but approachable instrument inside the editorial workspace. It should reveal the traffic signal an editor needs, then get out of the way. The visual language is friendly and conversational, but its structure is disciplined: familiar Umbraco controls, clear hierarchy, compact reporting, and feedback close to the action that caused it. The extension belongs to the backoffice rather than merely living inside it. It inherits Umbraco UI Library semantics, Lato typography, spacing, focus treatment, and surface hierarchy. Custom analytics patterns may borrow the clarity of Vercel Analytics, but they must be translated into Umbraco's interaction language. The system explicitly rejects the density, configuration burden, and specialist terminology of an overly dense enterprise analytics platform. It is not Google Analytics, and it must never feel like a separate third-party dashboard embedded in Umbraco.
keyCharacteristics: Native to Umbraco and visually continuous with the backoffice.
Compact, calm, and focused on decisions rather than exhaustive data.
Friendly in its language and precise in its numbers.
Progressive: common answers first, depth only on request.
Accessible through Umbraco conventions for keyboard, focus, contrast, and reduced motion.

rules.name: The Host Owns the Palette Rule
rules.body: Use semantic Umbraco UI variables in production components. Never hard-code a competing brand palette into the extension.

rules.name: The Rare Accent Rule
rules.body: Interactive Indigo identifies selection, focus, or a meaningful action. It must not become a decorative stripe or large background field.

rules.name: The Backoffice Voice Rule
rules.body: Use sentence case, plain language, and concise labels. Never import marketing-display typography or analytics jargon to create hierarchy.

rules.name: The Numbers Stay Still Rule
rules.body: Counts, percentages, and chart axes use tabular numerals and must not shift horizontally while data updates.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Dashboard cards and settings sections remain flat at rest. If every box casts a shadow, the hierarchy has failed.

dos: Do compose native uui-* elements and semantic --uui-color-*, --uui-size-*, and --uui-type-* variables.
Do lead with the traffic answer most relevant to the editor's current page or site.
Do keep default views compact and progressively reveal uncommon settings or reporting depth.
Do use friendly, conversational guidance close to the control or report it explains.
Do preserve visible focus, keyboard operation, screen-reader semantics, sufficient contrast, and reduced-motion behavior consistent with Umbraco.
Do reserve shadows for menus, dialogs, date pickers, and tooltips that overlay the current workspace.
donts: Don't reproduce the density, configuration burden, or specialist terminology of enterprise analytics platforms such as Google Analytics.
Don't imply that every available dimension or control must be visible at once.
Don't make the extension feel like a separate application or a third-party widget embedded inside Umbraco.
Don't invent a separate brand palette, typography system, or decorative gradient that competes with the backoffice.
Don't scatter save feedback, filters, or validation away from the component that owns them.
Don't use color alone for success, warning, failure, selection, or filtering state.
Don't animate charts on load or allow skeletons to change the final component height.
